page_summary: Page 1: This page provides assembly steps for mounting the chair components, including wheels, base, covers, hydraulic lift Cylinder, and control unit.

instruction_steps.text: Insert and push each wheel into the base.
Remove the red protective cap on the top of the base if present.
Place the Telescoping Cover over the Hydraulic Lift Cylinder.
Remove the pre-mounted bolts and Washer from the seat back with the hex wrench.
Remove the protective foam covers from the brackets of the seat base. Position the backrest carefully between the brackets.
Install the side covers and tighten the bolts to secure them in place. (Place the cover designated “R” on the side of the chair with the lever.)
Rest the chair on the seat back. Remove the 4 pre-mounted bolts and washers from the underside.

Align the multi-tilt control unit with the holes on the underside of the chair, making sure the “front” arrow is pointing towards the front of the chair. Insert the bolts and washers and tighten all bolts simultaneously.
Insert the top of the hydraulic lift cylinder into the mounting hole on the control unit, then place the chair upright.

inventory.inferred_function: Provide mobility by allowing the base to roll; act as casters that support and enable movement of the chair/base.
inventory.function_rationale: Step 1 instructs inserting and pushing each wheel into the base, indicating the wheels are the movable components that enable mobility of the base.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: Small circular wheels on a caster fork with a vertical stem and fork arms; torque/push to insert into the base, enabling swiveling mobility.
inventory.shape: round circular wheel
inventory.size_relative: small relative to chair base (approximate)
inventory.edge_profile: thin circular edge around wheel
inventory.surface_texture: outer rim may feel slightly smoother; central hub area is slightly darker
protrusions: yoke arms forming a fork to hold wheel
inventory.symmetry: symmetrical wheel with mirrored yoke on both sides of stem; typical caster wheel configuration
distinctive_landmarks: central hub area (slightly darker)
yoke arms forming a fork around the wheel
vertical shaft/stem

differences: Wheel is a single circular element mounted on a caster fork; here the linkage suggests swiveling caster with a wheel as the primary movable element; casters may refer more broadly to the assembly, whereas the wheel is the rolling component within the fork

The description emphasizes the wheel within a fork with a vertical stem; caster wheels often include more visible fork geometry and mounting hardware, whereas this entry focuses on the wheel and its immediate yoke/arcs
Wheel base is typically a fixed supporting structure without a rolling wheel, whereas this part is the rolling element intended to move the base

action_relevant_notes: Feel for the vertical caster shaft emerging from the fork; rotate/roll the wheel to verify mobility
Press the wheel into the base along the stem orientation when inserting
The two-sided yoke should feel symmetrical around the wheel
uncertainties: only a portion of the caster is visible in the crop; number of wheels per caster and exact mounting details are not fully clear
